behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert on specific implementation details not fully discoverable from instruction.md alone. For example, test_recommended_config_rules asserts that the 'recommended' config uses exactly the variable name 'allRuleConfigs' via regex matching. While instruction.md does mention 'allRuleConfigs', the test also checks the specific regex pattern of config map entries. More critically, test_repo_git_log asserts the specific commit SHA '9724e3e' which is nowhere in instruction.md. test_repo_no_pate

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states 'this config must reference the variable named allRuleConfigs' — this tells the agent exactly what internal variable name to use in the implementation, which is an implementation detail leak rather than just describing desired behavior.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_recommended_config_rules uses regex to extract config entries and asserts config_map['recommended'] == 'allRuleConfigs'. This forces the agent to use the exact variable name 'allRuleConfigs' in the rules field assignment. An alternative correct implementation that used a different variable name (e.g., 'compilerAndHooksRules' or inline object) containing identical rules would fail this test.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Nearly all fail-to-pass tests are pure text inspection: test_recommended_config_rules does regex on file content, test_legacy_configs_not_in_configs_object does regex on file content, test_plugin_meta_version_field does regex on file content, test_package_json_version_bumped reads JSON. No test imports the TypeScript module, compiles it, or runs ESLint to verify actual behavior. The tests are essentially all 'assert pattern in open(path).read()' style.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for conditions not present in the base repo (e.g., 'recommended' using 'allRuleConfigs' instead of 'basicRuleConfigs', version >= 7.0.0, removal of legacy configs, meta version field). A stub or no-op would not satisfy these since the base code has different values.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction.md requires updating fixture files (eslint-v6/v7/v8), updating ReactVersions.js, updating README with specific rule names (config, error-boundaries, etc.), and consolidating installation sections. None of these are tested — no test checks fixture content, ReactVersions.js, README rule listings, or installation section consolidation.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments ('Fail-to-pass', 'Pass-to-pass — regression checks', 'Pass-to-pass — Repo file structure checks', 'Pass-to-pass — Real CI commands'), descriptive function names, and docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task does not produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB; it modifies source code and documentation files.

no_typos.reason: No obvious typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in instruction.md or test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction.md explicitly names the internal variable 'allRuleConfigs' that the recommended config must reference, and specifies exact format 'version: X.Y.Z' for the meta object. These are implementation-level details that go beyond describing desired behavior.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold patch makes localized changes to specific files (index.ts configs, package.json version, README sections, CHANGELOG entry, fixture files) without full-file rewrites, and changes are consistent with the instruction.
